taxonomy: Tragacantha serotina is a type of thorny shrub in the pea family, Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Astragalus, which is a large and diverse group of plants. More specifically, Tragacantha serotina is classified as Astragalus serotinus, which is a species of milkvetch. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphology. Astragalus serotinus is a perennial plant native to Europe and Western Asia.
uses: Tragacantha serotina, also known as the scurf pea or scurf pea broom, has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been employed to treat various conditions such as wounds, ulcers, and skin irritations. It is also believed to have antifungal properties, which may help in preventing fungal infections. However, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
